improvements_list: §0.2.2 LLM Consumption Model: new formal section with constraint/mitigation table and pseudocode for LLM implementation correctness — grounds the three new invariants in first principles
INV-017 Negative Specification Coverage: new invariant requiring ≥3 DO NOT constraints per implementation chapter, with violation scenario and validation method
INV-018 Structural Redundancy at Point of Use: new invariant requiring invariants to be restated at point of use (not just referenced by ID), directly combating LLM context-loss failure mode
INV-019 Implementation Ordering Explicitness: new invariant requiring an explicit DAG for implementation ordering with stated reasons per edge
ADR-008 LLM Provisions Woven Throughout: new ADR recording the architectural decision not to isolate LLM guidance in a separate chapter
ADR-009 Negative Specifications as Formal Elements: new ADR establishing §3.8 as a required structural element rather than optional guidance
ADR-010 Verification Prompts per Implementation Chapter: new ADR establishing §5.6 as a required structural element
§3.8 Negative Specifications element specification: new full element spec with required format, quality criteria, quantity guidance, anti-patterns, and self-bootstrapping demonstration
§5.6 Verification Prompts element specification: new full element spec with required format including positive/negative/integration check structure and concrete self-bootstrapping demonstration
§5.7 Meta-Instructions element specification: new full element spec defining when and how to embed process directives for LLM implementers
Gate 7 LLM Implementation Readiness: new quality gate with concrete thought-experiment demonstration applied to §3.4 — operationalizes the new LLM invariants

§1.4 End-to-End Trace in PART I: new worked example tracing ADR-002 through the full DDIS authoring process, fulfilling both the PART I trace requirement and ADR-004 self-bootstrapping validation; state machine in §1.1 now fully satisfies INV-010 with guards, entry actions, and explicit invalid-transition policies; violation scenarios added to INV-001 through INV-010 fixing v0's own INV-003 self-conformance violation; DO NOT annotations woven throughout all element specifications in PART II; §0.8.4 adds measurable LLM hallucination rate metric; new non-negotiable 'Negative specifications prevent hallucination'; causal chain diagram extended with Negative Specifications and Verification Prompts layers
remaining_gaps: Self-demonstration of §5.6 verification prompts is incomplete: individual element specs in PART II define 'DO NOT' constraints (demonstrating §3.8) but most lack explicit structured verification prompt blocks as required by INV-017 and §5.6 — the spec defines verification prompts without systematically applying them to its own element specs

PART III and PART IV not verified for LLM-woven updates: ADR-008 mandates that every element specification include woven LLM guidance, but the voice guidance chapter (§8.1), anti-pattern catalog (§8.3), and operational playbook chapters were not reached in this review and may not yet carry the new DO NOT pattern

Appendix C (Specification Error Taxonomy) and Appendix D (Quick-Reference Card) are declared in the document structure but their content was not verified — high risk they are placeholder stubs rather than fully authored appendices, which would violate Gate 1 Structural Conformance

ADR supersession protocol remains an unresolved Open Question (§0.10.3 in v1): how a DDIS spec handles evolved or overturned ADRs has no prescribed procedure, leaving living specs with no formal evolution mechanism

INV-018 self-conformance partial: while the implementation chapter template (§5.1) now mandates invariant restating, the DDIS standard's own PART 0 invariant sections are not restated in the element specification chapters that describe how to write them — a minor but verifiable self-conformance gap
rationale: v1 delivers 12 substantive improvements over v0, correcting v0's own INV-003 self-conformance violation (missing violation scenarios on its own invariants) and adding three formally grounded new invariants, three new ADRs, three new required element specifications, and a Gate 7. The score advances from approximately 74 to 84 — a 10-point improvement well above the continue threshold. The remaining gaps (incomplete self-demonstration of §5.6, unverified PART III/IV LLM updates, unconfirmed Appendix C/D content) are all addressable in a subsequent iteration without structural redesign.
